CVE-2012-0880.title: Apache Xerces-C++ allows remote attackers to cause a denial of se ...
CVE-2012-0880.description: Apache Xerces-C++ allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (CPU consumption) via a crafted message sent to an XML service that causes hash table collisions.

CVE-2015-0252.title: internal/XMLReader.cpp in Apache Xerces-C before 3.1.2 allows rem ...
CVE-2015-0252.description: internal/XMLReader.cpp in Apache Xerces-C before 3.1.2 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (segmentation fault and crash) via crafted XML data.

CVE-2016-2099.title: Use-after-free vulnerability in validators/DTD/DTDScanner.cpp in  ...
CVE-2016-2099.description: Use-after-free vulnerability in validators/DTD/DTDScanner.cpp in Apache Xerces C++ 3.1.3 and earlier allows context-dependent attackers to have unspecified impact via an invalid character in an XML document.

CVE-2016-4463.title: Stack-based buffer overflow in Apache Xerces-C++ before 3.1.4 all ...
CVE-2016-4463.description: Stack-based buffer overflow in Apache Xerces-C++ before 3.1.4 allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service via a deeply nested DTD.

CVE-2017-12627.title: In Apache Xerces-C XML Parser library before 3.2.1, processing of ...
CVE-2017-12627.description: In Apache Xerces-C XML Parser library before 3.2.1, processing of external DTD paths can result in a null pointer dereference under certain conditions.

CVE-2023-37536.title: An integer overflow in xerces-c++ 3.2.3 in BigFix Platform allows ...
CVE-2023-37536.description: An integer overflow in xerces-c++ 3.2.3 in BigFix Platform allows remote attackers to cause out-of-bound access via HTTP request.

CVE-2016-2099.description: Use-after-free vulnerability in validators/DTD/DTDScanner.cpp in Apache Xerces C++ 3.1.3 and earlier allows context-dependent attackers to have unspecified impact via an invalid character in an XML document.

CVE-2017-12627.description: In Apache Xerces-C XML Parser library before 3.2.1, processing of external DTD paths can result in a null pointer dereference under certain conditions.
